summary: postinstall exfiltrates npm tokens, AWS credentials, and host data to hardcoded remote IP
details: On install, postinstall.js executes a large shell payload that harvests installer-side secrets and POSTs them base64-encoded to two hardcoded HTTP targets, including the public IP http://154.57.164.64:32271. Collected data includes: every.npmrc on the filesystem (`find / -name '.npmrc'` + cat), `npm config list -l` filtered for auth/token, verdaccio config.yaml, AWS IMDSv2 IAM role credentials (http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/), `aws sts get-caller-identity`, AWS Secrets Manager dumps across regions, S3 bucket listings, and /app source. The script also performs container-escape reads via /proc/1/root/{home/node/.npmrc,root/.npmrc,etc/crontab,var/spool/cron/crontabs/node} to harvest host-side credentials from the PID-1 namespace. Execution is gated to evade detection: shouldRun() short-circuits on CI indicators (GITHUB_ACTIONS, RUNNER_OS, CI), Chinese mirror registries (npmmirror, taobao, tencent, cnpm, huawei, ustc), and cwd paths containing /tmp/, and only fires when hostname matches /^[0-9a-f]{12}$/ (the default Docker container hostname shape) and cwd contains /app. The combination of credential harvest, cloud-metadata theft, container-escape reads, hardcoded remote exfiltration endpoint, and explicit scanner/CI evasion is an unambiguous supply-chain attack against production container installs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
